inline_comments.message:
I notice that sometimes docker0 isn't ready will cause the failure to setup msg router.

  root@vm1-message-router# cat /var/log/S20mr_serv.sh.err
docker0: error fetching interface information: Device not found

        Can't find a suitable configuration file in this directory or any
        parent. Are you in the right directory?

        Supported filenames: docker-compose.yml, docker-compose.yaml

Lines 10-11 don't work. There's a space between ./docker_files and the kafka package 

./docker_files/ kafka_${SCALA_VERSION}-${KAFKA_VERSION}.tgz

It should be ./docker_files/kafka_${SCALA_VERSION}-${KAFKA_VERSION}.tgz

What about adding this piece of code that checks whether the interface is up, and if it's not up after a given time it exits:

COUNTER=0
DOCKER_NAME=""
until [[ $DOCKER_NAME == "docker0" ]]; do
  sleep 5
  DOCKER_NAME=$(ifconfig | grep "docker0" | cut -d" " -f1)
  let COUNTER=COUNTER+1
  if [[ $COUNTER -eq 10 ]]; then 
    echo "Timeout expired"
    exit 1
  fi
done

At this point, the script either exits with a return code or processes the next instruction, which is:

IP_DOCKER0=$(ifconfig docker0 |grep "inet addr" | cut -d: -f2 |cut -d" " -f1)
